macro_segments.title: Assessment of Boshet (Embarrassment Damages) and the Debate over Sela Tzori versus Sela Medina

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna on Boshet and the Question of Which Sela is Referenced
Rav Tuvia bar Matna's Question to Rav Yosef: Does Mishna Mean Sela Tzori or Sela Medina?
Inference from the Goring Ox Mishna: Why Didn't the Tanna Mention Half-Zuz Amounts?
Resolution: Biblical Amounts Use Sela Tzori; Rabbinic Assessments Use Sela Medina
The Case of Chanan Bisha: Unable to Exchange a Worn Coin for Half-Zuz
Tosafot on How Words Transfer Property: The Limits of Verbal Obligation for Tzedaka

macro_segments.title: The Mishna on Muad (Habitually Vicious Animals) and the Debate Between Rav Zevid and Rav Pappa over Generalization of Patterns

micro_segments.title: Mishna: A Muad Animal Established for Its Kind But Not for Other Kinds; When Does It Revert to Tam?
Question to Rabbi Yehuda: If an Animal Gores Only on Shabbat, Is It Muad Only for Shabbat?
Textual Debate: Does Mishna Have Vav (Descriptive) or Not (Prescriptive)? Rav Zevid vs. Rav Pappa Reading
Rav Zevid's Position: Animals Generalize Unless Evidence Shows They Don't; Rav Pappa's Position: Only Proven Pattern Applies
Gemara Evidence from Mishna Structure: Analysis of the Final Clause and What It Implies
Summoning a Baraita of Sumchus: If Muad for Adam, Then Muad for Behema by Kal VaChomer
Rav Zevid's Resolution: The Debate Is Over Reversion (Teshuva), Not Initial Establishment
The Case of Shabbat vs. Weekdays Revisited: Does the Vav Make It Descriptive or Prescriptive?

macro_segments.title: Pattern Identification in Muad: How to Determine the Scope of a Vicious Animal's Habit Within Rav Pappa's Framework

micro_segments.title: Example: If Animal Gored Ox, Donkey, and Camel, Does It Become Muad for All or Only for Proven Pattern?
Alternating Pattern: If Animal Gores on Alternate Goring Events, It Becomes Muad L'Sirugin (Alternately)
Unresolved Questions (Teiku): Does First Three Gorings or Last Three Determine the Pattern?
Date-Based Patterns: 15th, 16th, 17th of Each Month; Debate Between Rav and Shmuel
Application of Pattern Logic: Shofar Example and the Principle That Any Three Instances Establish Muad
Conclusion: Formalist Approach to Pattern Identification Without Causal Explanation
